calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-19-2021 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Up Prove-Up Hearing And For Entry Of Court Judgment Against Defendants Ckr Law, Llp, Ckr Law - Los Angeles, And Ckr Law - New York
Off Calendar Jan-04-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for a Specially Set Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendants CKR Law, LLP, CKR - Law Los Angeles and CKR Law - New York.
Dept. 514. Judge: Murlene J. Randle. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-551-3680. Email: gcasey@sftc.org.

Kaipo K.B. Young, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Niesar & Vestal LLP.
Gerald Niesar, a partner at Neiser & Vestal LLP and witness for plaintiff, was placed under oath and testified.
Both Messrs. Young and Niesar appeared via ZOOM for this morning's hearing.
No appearance by defendants CKR Law, LLP, CKR - Law Los Angeles and CKR Law - New York.
Plaintiff's Complaint for Breach of Contract and Common Counts was filed on May 8, 2019.
Substitute service of plaintiff's summons, complaint and accompanying documents on defendants CKR Law, LLP, CKR - Law Los Angeles and CKR Law - New York occurred on September 23, 2019 and was effectuated on October 5, 2019.
Default as to all three defendants was entered on January 7, 2020.
DOE defendants were dismissed on December 17, 2020.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff Niesar & Vestal LLP, and against the defaulted defendants CKR Law, LLP, CKR - Law Los Angeles and CKR Law - New York, as follows:

Damages/Principal - $36,689.81.
Prejudgment Interest - $5,999.85.
Costs - $739.00.

Total - $43,428.66.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
